title: SSE CRLF normalization allocates a new String every network chunk
description: In subscribe_events, after each received chunk the entire buffer is replaced via buf.replace(CRLF, LF).replace(CR, LF). This allocates a new String proportional to the total buffered bytes on every network read. For a long-lived SSE connection with many small chunks, this is a consistent O(n) allocation where n is the accumulated buffer size. The pattern will be copied by contributors adding other streaming features. The 1 MiB cap prevents the pathological case but does not address the per-chunk allocation.
